rulings.Case Title: THE ALLIANCE 1849 LLC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER THE ALLIANCE 1849 LLC'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The motion was timely filed. The statutory requirement of motions to compel within 45 days of service of a verified discovery response does not apply when a non-verified objection-only pleading is served. C.C.P. 2030.300(c). The Court declines to follow the federal court approach to discovery in FOIA matters and will instead rule on whether the discovery in dispute is likely to aid in the resolution of the issues raised in the proceeding. See City of Los Angeles vs. Superior Court 9 Cal.App.5th 272. The Court will hear argument and rule on each of the discovery objections in Respondent's responsive pleading. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER NOELLE BONNER'S Notice Of Hearing on petition to confirm arbitration Petitioners Noelle Bonner and The NOBO LLC's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. The NOBO LLC is entitled to judgment in its favor and against respondent Applepalm Enterprises Inc. in the amount of $27,562.16 plus costs per the memorandum of costs procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Mr. Bonner is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS VS. BETTY YEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Thrivent Financial For Lutherans For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF THRIVENT FINANCIAL FOR LUTHERANS' Motion Of Plaintiff Thrivent Financial For Lutherans For Attorneys Fees Defendant Thrivent Financial for Lutherans' motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Even considering all of the evidence submitted with the reply papers, Thrivent has not shown that the judgment in its favor conferred a significant benefit on the general public or a large class of persons, which is a required element for the award of attorneys' fees per CCP 1021.5. Specifically, Thrivent has not shown that any life insurer other than it or any insureds or beneficiaries of life insurance policies issued by another life insurer has benefitted in any significant way by the judgment issued in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the SCO is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Further Response And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANTS TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD, ZHONGLI NEW ENERGY USA CO., LLC'S Motion To Compel A Further Response And For Sanctions Defendants Talesun Solar USA Ltd.'s motion to compel a further response to document requests is granted. No later than January 31, 2019 plaintiff Martifer-Silverado Fund 1, LLC must: a) produce all non-privileged documents responsive to Talesun's third set of document requests that it has not previously produced to Talesun in this case; b) provide a privilege log identifying each document responsive to Talesun's third set of document requests that it has withheld on any ground of privilege; and c) serve further verified responses stating that all non-privileged responsive documents have previously been produced or are being produced concurrent with the further responses. Martifer's failure to unequivocally state in verified responses that it previously produced and/or will produce by a date certain all non-privileged responsive documents lacks substantial justification and warrants imposition of monetary sanctions. The high end of a reasonable range of monetary sanctions for this exceedingly simple motion is $2000. Martifer must pay that amount to Talesun no later than February 15, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Talesun is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA ISABEL DELGADO VS. PRIMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO INSURANCE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Wells Fargo Insurance, Inc.'s motion to strike the amendment to the complaint filed October 29, 2018 or, in the alternative, to strike specified portions of the complaint is denied in its entirety. WFI's request for judicial notice of portions of plaintiff Maria Delgado's deposition testimony is denied. While the parties appear to view the judicial notice issue as either the content of all deposition testimony of a party that is inconsistent with the party's pleading is judicially noticeable or the content of a party's deposition testimony is never judicially noticeable, this court adopts the more nuanced and better-reasoned "third approach" set forth in Joslin v. H.A.S. Insurance Brokerage (1986) 184 Cal. App. 369, 375 that deposition testimony of a pleader is judicially noticeable only "to the extent that ¿ [the statements in the deposition] were not or could not be disputed." Because Ms. Delgado vigorously disputes that she verified the discovery responses that were served in October 2018 and WFI initially received consent for the purchase of the National Union insurance, application of third approach requires denial of WFI's request for judicial notice. The denial of WFI's request for judicial notice also requires the denial of the motion. Ms. Delgado's request for sanctions per CCP 128.5 is denied because WFI's motion had some arguable legal and factual support and thus was not frivolous and there is no basis to conclude that the motion was filed solely for the purposes of delay when the motion did not and could not delay the pre-existing trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Delgado is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN DUONG HUANG ET AL VS. HUI WU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANTS HUI WU, MICHAEL YOUNG Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendants Hui Wu and Michael Young's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Susan Huang and Que Duong is denied as to all four causes of action. Judge Chan's order filed May 29, 2018 does not bar any of the four claims alleged by Ms. Huang and Ms. Duong in this case either based on res judicata or collateral estoppel. Res judicata is inapplicable because this case does not involve either the same cause of action or remedy that was involved in the family law case. Collateral estoppel is inapplicable because none of the issues essential to plaintiffs prevailing on any of their claims in this case were conclusively litigated and decided in the family law case. Judge Chan decided that a restraining order was unwarranted, the request for a restraining order was filed with an ulterior motive, and Ms. Huang's testimony lacked credibility. Judge Chan did not, however, conclusively determine that no assault or abuse occurred. Liberally construing the complaint, plaintiffs have adequately alleged claims for assault, intentional infliction of emotional distress, and elder abuse, which are not uncertain, ambiguous, or unintelligible. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARION JAMES MARCIN VS. CCMH FISHERMAN'S WHARF LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 10, DEFENDANTS CCMH FISHERMAN'S WHARF LLC,, HOST HOTEL & RESORTS, INC., HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, L.P., BARCELO CRESTLINE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants CCMH Fisherman's Wharf LLC, Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc., Host Hotels & Resorts, L.P., and Barcelo Crestline Corporation's motion to strike portions of the complaint filed by plaintiffs Marion, Brian and Sharri Marcin is: a) granted without leave to amend as to the words "and physical injuries, all of" and "physical and" in lines 22 and 23 on page 7; b) granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages but without prejudice to seeking leave to amend to add a prayer for punitive damages in the event that the Marcins obtain facts supporting punitive damages liability against the moving defendants; and c) denied as to all other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken. The Marcins agree to delete the words "and physical injuries, all of" and "physical and" in lines 22 and 23 on page 7. The complaint does not allege sufficient ultimate facts showing that any of the moving defendants acted despicably. Penner v. Falk (1984) 153 Cal. App. 3d 858 does not support the sufficiency of the Marcins' prayer for punitive damages. Unlike Penner, the Marcins have not alleged any facts showing long-existing conditions portending danger that were ignored by the moving defendants. Perhaps even more significantly, subsequent to Penner Civil Code 3294 was amended to make it harder to obtain punitive damages by, among other things, requiring that a defendant engage in despicable conduct for punitive damages based on malice and oppression. None of the other words sought to be stricken are so obviously irrelevant or abusive that they should be removed from the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for moving defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TAMMIE MOSLEY VS. CHASE HEATON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANTS CHASE HEATON, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendants Chase Heaton and the Regents of the University of California's motion to strike punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tammie Mosley is granted with three months leave to amend as to Dr. Heaton and granted without leave to amend as to the Regents. Punitive damages are not available against The Regents. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Mosley may file a first amended complaint no later than April 15, 2019 which seeks punitive damages against Dr. Heaton that are not prohibited by CCP 425.13. If Ms. Mosley does not file a first amended complaint by April 15, 2019, then the complaint without any punitive damages allegations will be the operative complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the UC defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TAMMIE MOSLEY VS. CHASE HEATON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT CHASE HEATON, THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. Defendants Chase Heaton and the Regents of the University of California's demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional tort in the complaint filed by plaintiff Tammie Mosley is sustained with three months leave to amend. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Mosley may file a first amended complaint no later than April 15, 2019 which alleges any viable claim in addition to her first cause of action for medical negligence. Ms. Mosley may wish to use the three months to try to retain counsel so that she may allege claims on behalf of her children. Ms. Mosley may wish to contact the Bar Association of San Francisco regarding retention of counsel. If Ms. Mosley does not file a first amended complaint by April 15, 2019, then the complaint without the second cause of action will be the operative complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: KELLEY CARLIN VS. WRIGHT GARDNER LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT WRIGHT GARDNER LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Wright Gardner LLC's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiff Kelley Carlin is denied. Ms. Carlin adequately alleges that Wright Gardner engaged in despicable conduct constituting unlawful discrimination, retaliation, and harassment that was committed and/or ratified by Mr. Haschka, which is sufficient to support a prayer for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: KELLEY CARLIN VS. WRIGHT GARDNER LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 15, 2019, Line 12, DEFENDANT WRIGHT GARDNER LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Wright Gardner LLC's demurrer to the first cause of action for discrimination, the fifth cause of action for retaliation and the sixth cause of action for harassment in the complaint filed by plaintiff Kelley Carlin is overruled as to all three causes of action. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that she was subject to unfavorable disparate treatment based on her gender, disability and age including the termination of her employment, which is sufficient for a FEHA discrimination claim. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that her employment was terminated because she complained to Mr. Haschka that she had been unfavorably disparately treated due to her gender, which is sufficient for a FEHA retaliation claim. Ms. Carlin adequately alleged that she was subjected to repeated unwelcome intolerable behavior by Mr. Figueroa that included numerous derogatory comments reflecting on her disability, which is sufficient to allege severe and/or pervasive misconduct for a FEHA harassment claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Carlin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
